payload.answer: mem_new was returned because recall prefers the current, non-superseded memory for the resolved Sam-in-Goldman context. mem_old was filtered because it is older and has been superseded by mem_new, so showing it as current would violate the rule against surfacing superseded facts as active memory.
payload.decision: Recall returned mem_new as the current applicable memory for Sam in the Goldman context and filtered mem_old because mem_old is marked or treated as superseded by mem_new.

entity_resolution.reason: The recall path appears to treat the Sam referenced by mem_new and mem_old in the Goldman context as the same underlying entity, so conflict handling was applied within one entity timeline rather than across separate people.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new was selected because it is the active/current memory for the resolved Sam entity in the Goldman context.
mem_old was excluded from recall because it is superseded by mem_new and should not be presented as the current fact.

details: Explained why mem_new was eligible for recall.
Explained why mem_old was filtered as superseded.
Preserved ambiguity about the exact fact content while describing the recall logic.

repair_options: Verify that mem_old has an explicit superseded_by link to mem_new.
Check timestamp/recency ordering if supersession is inferred rather than explicitly stored.
Confirm entity resolution did not incorrectly merge two different people named Sam.
Inspect whether the Goldman context constraint matched both memories and then correctly preferred the active one.
If both memories should remain visible, change recall mode to include historical/superseded records rather than current-only recall.
